evidence_quotes.quote: the disabling of online software packages (or services) at the convenience of the software provider
the requirement of money to cancel an online subscription (as adobe is doing)
"Do Not Track" Headers mandatory to follow

paragraphs.text: Hello :) This proposal is absolutely hitting the nail in the head and it is very important to try and regulate companies online. Please do not though try to undermine privacy and freedom of speech/expression as America and the UK are doing now... Also I would like to add 1. the disabling of online software packages (or services) at the convenience of the software provider and 2. the requirement of money to cancel an online subscription (as adobe is doing). The latter one should be good to include into the "addictive design of digital products" point, as it kind of prevents users from canceling the software package (they also disable the retirement of the subscription when the time period runs out) If you are feeling really ambitions, then you could also regulate online tracking (with maybe making "Do Not Track" Headers mandatory to follow) or promoting online privacy to the status of a Humanright or equivalent in the EU and ensuring it will not be violated.
